MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID W. CHEN OF THE STRATMAN, PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. (510) 457-3442 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT ADSUAR;
ATTY. DAVID A. DELBON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3962 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT CCSF.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Cross-Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF") request for judicial notice is granted. CCSF has not met its initial burden of showing a complete defense to Cross-Complainant Adsuar's First Amended Cross-Complaint. CCSF has not produced admissible evidence demonstrating that Stanton Street was not accepted by the Board of Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco. (Declaration of Cisneros In Support of CCSF's Motion For Summary Judgment 3, Exh. B; see also California Streets & Highway Code, 941(b).) Cross-Complainant Adsuar's objections to the Declaration of Mr. Cisneros are sustained. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Off calendar per the Order after hearing dated October 29, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Motion For Leave To Amend X-Complaint To Add Cause Of Action For Statutory Indemnity And Contribution Pursuant To Ccp 473
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FREDERICK A. PATTERSON OF STRATMAN, PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. (510) 457-3473 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANT PATRICK ADSUAR's Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Add Cause Of Action For Statutory Indemnity And Contribution Pursuant To CCP 473 is Granted. (CCP 473(a).) There is a strong policy favoring liberality in amending pleadings. Further, the Court finds that the delay has not prejudiced CCSF, and can be ameliorated by a continuance of the trial date. Mr. Adsuar is granted leave to file and serve a First Amended Cross-Complaint within 5 days after notice of entry of order. The new allegations do not change new theories of recovery not raised in his tort claim; Mr. Adsuar specifically requested indemnity and contribution in his tort claim. (Delbon Decl., Exhibit B.) The trial date of December 2, 2013 is vacated and a new trial date is set for March 17, 2014. The new trial date will be treated as the initial trial date for all discovery and pre-trial statutory deadlines. Defendant's motion for summary judgment, now set for hearing on November 1, 2013, will be vacated in light of this ruling.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Suzanne Crozier, CSR # 4761 =(302/MJM)
